items.headline: Mali defense minister killed as separatists and al-Qaida-linked militants jointly attack five cities
items.body: Defence Minister Sadio Camara died when coordinated attacks hit military sites nationwide on April 25-26. Tuareg separatists announced Russian Africa Corps and Malian forces withdrew from Kidal following the assault.

items.headline: Meta to cut 10 percent of workforce affecting 8,000 employees on May 20
items.body: The company will eliminate those positions and leave 6,000 roles unfilled. Meta spent over 72 billion dollars on infrastructure in 2025 to support AI development.

items.headline: Colombia bombing kills 20 on highway bus as rebel attacks multiply across southwest
items.body: An explosive device detonated on a Pan-American Highway bus in Cajibio on Saturday. At least 26 separate incidents occurred across the region within two days.

items.headline: Uganda President Museveni wins seventh term extending four-decade rule
items.body: Official results announced Saturday confirmed the former guerrilla's victory in East Africa's third most populous country. Opposition leader Bobi Wine remains in hiding after security forces raided his residence.

uk.headline: Parliament passes smartphone ban in schools with legal force after Lords approves government amendment
uk.body: Education minister Baroness Smith tabled the amendment to the Children's Wellbeing and Schools Bill on Monday. Schools must now enforce phone-free days rather than follow voluntary guidance.
